name: security-patterns
description: Web security: XSS prevention, CSRF, SQL injection, Content Security Policy, input sanitization, secrets management

entries.title: XSS Prevention Strategies
entries.content: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS): attacker injects scripts via user input. Prevention: 1) React auto-escapes JSX (avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML), 2) Sanitize HTML with DOMPurify, 3) Set Content-Security-Policy header, 4) Use httpOnly cookies, 5) Validate/encode outputs.

entries.title: CSRF Protection Patterns
entries.content: Cross-Site Request Forgery: attacker tricks user's browser into making requests. Prevention: 1) SameSite=Lax cookies (default modern browsers), 2) CSRF tokens in forms, 3) Check Origin/Referer headers, 4) Use custom headers for APIs.

entries.title: Input Validation & Sanitization
entries.content: Validate ALL inputs server-side. Whitelist over blacklist. Use schema validation (Zod). Sanitize for context (HTML, SQL, URLs). Limit string lengths. Reject unexpected fields.

entries.title: Secrets & Environment Variables
entries.content: Never commit secrets to git. Use .env files locally, env vars in production. Validate required env vars at startup. Use NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix only for client-safe values. Rotate secrets regularly.

entries.title: Security Headers Configuration
entries.content: Essential security headers: Strict-Transport-Security (HSTS), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy. Set in next.config.js or middleware.
